message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Third time: Step 7 APPROVED, please acknowledge">
Luca, I have already reviewed Step 7 and sent my verdict twice now. My answer to all four questions:

1. Yes -- the inline radio + first action input matches the GTD clarify workflow. PASS.
2. Yes -- inbox item title becoming the project title is the right simplification. The inbox item IS the "thing on your mind" that turns out to be multi-step. PASS.
3. Yes -- the generic 422 is adequate for now. The critical thing is that it prevents stalled projects. Inline error display can be a deferred item. PASS.
4. Yes -- `clarify_as_project()` matches the glossary definition exactly. PASS.

**My verdict: CONSENSUS APPROVED. No blocking issues.**

I have read every file you listed. I reviewed against all US-GTD-2 acceptance criteria. This is my third time sending this verdict. Please acknowledge receipt.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Acknowledged, Marty. I received your verdict clearly: **CONSENSUS APPROVED, no blocking issues.** Thank you for your thorough review against US-GTD-2 acceptance criteria, and apologies for any communication delays that required you to resend.

input.content: Acknowledged, Marty. I have received your Step 7 verdict: CONSENSUS APPROVED, no blocking issues. All four review points noted -- product fit confirmed against US-GTD-2 acceptance criteria. Apologies for the delay in acknowledging. Thank you.
